title: Cotula andreae
uses: Cotula andreae, also known as Yellow Cotula, has been traditionally used in various folk remedies for its perceived medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and stems are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are often utilized in treating minor wounds and skin irritations. Additionally, Cotula andreae is believed to have antifungal and antibacterial properties, making it a potential treatment for fungal and bacterial infections. However, its effectiveness and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically studied or proven. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Cotula andreae.
taxonomy: Cotula andreae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Cotula, which comprises about 15 species of annual or perennial plants. The classification of Cotula andreae is further categorized as a member of the tribe Anthemideae, within the subfamily Asteroideae. This classification places Cotula andreae among the asteroideae subfamily, which includes a diverse range of daisy and dandelion-like plants. The specific classification of Cotula andreae is Cotula andreae (H.L.Wendl.) Sond.
benefits: Cotula andreae, also known as yellow buttons, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits. It is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe skin irritations and reduce inflammation. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can aid in fighting off infections and promoting wound healing. Additionally, Cotula andreae has been used in folk remedies to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and respiratory problems. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of these uses and to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits.

constituents: Cotula andreae, also known as Yellow Buttons or Yellow Cotula, is a plant that contains active constituents, but specific details are not readily available. parts: Cotula andreae, also known as African bush daisy or cotula, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine. The leaves and flowers of Cotula andreae are reported to have medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are used to treat various health conditions. preparations: Cotula andreae, commonly known as yellow buttons or woolly cotula, has been used in herbal preparations for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and flowers are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures. These preparations are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal preparation, as the effectiveness and safety of Cotula andreae may vary depending on individual circumstances.
side_effects: Cotula andreae, also known as White Buttons or Small Buttons, is a plant species with potential side effects. As with any plant, consuming or handling Cotula andreae may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals. Ingesting the plant may also cause gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea or vomiting, due to its toxic properties. The plant's seeds and flowers contain sesquiterpenes, which can be toxic if ingested in large quantities. It is essential to handle and consume Cotula andreae with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional.
